title: Microsoft Makes Major Changes to Xbox Business
subtitle: The company cuts thousands of jobs and shifts focus toward its biggest game franchises

summary.en: Microsoft is undergoing a significant restructuring of its Xbox division to improve financial health. The company plans to lay off 3,200 employees and close or sell four studios. Resources are being moved toward high-priority projects like the Fallout series. Industry experts suggest these changes reflect a broader shift in Microsoft's strategy. The future of the Xbox brand remains a subject of intense debate among market analysts.
summary.zh: 微軟正對其 Xbox 部門進行重大重組，以改善財務狀況。該公司計畫裁員三千兩百人，並關閉或出售四間工作室。資源正被轉移至如《異塵餘生》系列等高優先級項目。產業專家認為，這些變動反映了微軟策略的廣泛轉向。Xbox 品牌的未來，在市場分析師之間仍是激烈辯論的主題。

paragraphs.en: Microsoft is currently moving forward with a major "reset" of its Xbox gaming division. This process involves significant job losses and a change in how the company manages its game studios. According to reports, the company plans to lay off a total of 3,200 staff members. These changes are part of a wider effort to focus resources on "higher priority projects" after the company admitted that its gaming business was "not healthy."
paragraphs.zh: 微軟（Microsoft）目前正推動其 Xbox 遊戲部門的重大「重整」。此過程涉及大規模裁員，並改變了公司管理遊戲工作室的方式。據報導，該公司計畫裁減總計三千兩百名員工。這些變動是為了將資源集中於「更高優先級項目」的廣泛計畫之一，此前微軟已坦承其遊戲業務「並不健康」。

paragraphs.en: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma explained in a company memo that the business had previously "spread ourselves too thin." To fix this, Microsoft is moving away from a wide variety of projects to focus on its most popular and successful game franchises. As part of this new strategy, Obsidian Entertainment—a studio owned by Microsoft—is changing its development plans. According to Bloomberg, Obsidian has canceled several projects, including a planned sequel to the game Avowed, to begin work on a new title in the popular Fallout series. Josh Sawyer, a well-known director at the studio, will lead this new project.
paragraphs.zh: Xbox 執行長夏爾馬（Asha Sharma）在公司備忘錄中解釋，該業務先前「戰線拉得太長」。為了改善現狀，微軟正減少多樣化的專案，轉而專注於最受歡迎且成功的遊戲系列。作為這項新策略的一部分，微軟旗下的黑曜石娛樂（Obsidian Entertainment）正在調整其開發計畫。根據彭博社報導，黑曜石娛樂已取消多項計畫，包括原定的《宣誓》（Avowed）續作，轉而投入熱門《異塵餘生》（Fallout）系列新作的開發。該工作室知名總監索耶（Josh Sawyer）將領導此項新計畫。

paragraphs.en: This shift comes at a time when the Fallout brand is seeing renewed interest, partly due to the success of its television series. However, the decision to cancel other projects has been difficult for staff. Kotaku reported that about 25 percent of the employees at Obsidian were let go during this restructuring process. While Microsoft is narrowing its focus, it still maintains a large number of studios and properties, including Halo, Minecraft, Call of Duty, and Gears of War. The company has also teased a next-generation console, currently known by the project name "Project Helix."
paragraphs.zh: 此項轉變正值《異塵餘生》品牌因影集成功而重獲關注之際。然而，取消其他計畫的決定對員工而言相當艱難。遊戲媒體 Kotaku 報導稱，在此次重組過程中，黑曜石娛樂約有百分之二十五的員工遭到解僱。儘管微軟縮減了業務重心，但仍保有大量工作室與資產，包括《最後一戰》（Halo）、《當個創世神》（Minecraft）、《決勝時刻》（Call of Duty）以及《戰爭機器》（Gears of War）。該公司也預告了下一代主機，目前代號為「螺旋計畫」（Project Helix）。

paragraphs.en: The scale of these changes has led to questions about the long-term future of the Xbox brand. Some analysts wonder if Microsoft might eventually decide to sell the Xbox business entirely. Joost van Dreunen, a professor at New York University, told The Verge that a "wholesale divestiture"—which means selling off the entire business—remains a possibility. He noted that this seems more likely now because of rising costs for hardware and Microsoft’s increasing focus on artificial intelligence (AI) and other infrastructure projects. However, van Dreunen also pointed out that a full sale is still the "less likely path" because of the massive size and complexity of the Xbox business.
paragraphs.zh: 這些變動的規模引發了外界對於 Xbox 品牌長期未來的質疑。部分分析師好奇微軟最終是否會決定完全出售 Xbox 業務。紐約大學教授范德魯南（Joost van Dreunen）向科技媒體 The Verge 表示，「全面撤資」（即出售整個業務）仍是一種可能性。他指出，由於硬體成本上升，加上微軟日益重視人工智慧（AI）與其他基礎設施項目，這種可能性似乎有所增加。不過，范德魯南也強調，由於 Xbox 業務規模龐大且複雜，完全出售仍是「可能性較低的路徑」。

paragraphs.en: Finding a buyer for such a large operation would be difficult. The Xbox division generates over $23 billion in annual revenue, making it an expensive purchase for any company. While large tech firms like Amazon or Netflix might have the money, it is unclear if they would want to take on such a large interactive-entertainment business. Yoshio Osaki, the CEO of the market research firm IDG Intelligence, noted that while all options might be on the table, the current market environment makes a large purchase less likely. He observed that other major companies, such as Tencent and NetEase, have recently been stepping back from large investments in the gaming industry rather than expanding.
paragraphs.zh: 要為如此龐大的業務找到買家相當困難。Xbox 部門每年的營收超過兩百三十億美元，對任何公司而言都是一筆昂貴的收購。雖然像亞馬遜（Amazon）或網飛（Netflix）等大型科技公司可能有足夠資金，但並不清楚他們是否願意接手如此龐大的互動娛樂業務。市場研究公司 IDG Intelligence 的執行長尾崎（Yoshio Osaki）指出，儘管所有選項可能都在考慮範圍內，但目前的市場環境使得大型收購案發生的機率降低。他觀察到，其他大型企業如騰訊（Tencent）與網易（NetEase），近期對於遊戲產業的態度已從擴張轉為減少大規模投資。

paragraphs.en: For now, Microsoft is attempting to stabilize its position by cutting costs and focusing on its strongest assets. The company has not yet provided a clear long-term strategy for how Xbox fits into its overall business, which is increasingly focused on AI. As Microsoft continues this "reset," the gaming industry remains focused on whether these drastic measures will be enough to make the Xbox division profitable again. For the employees and fans of these game studios, the coming months will be a period of significant uncertainty as the company continues to reorganize its operations.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，微軟正試圖透過削減成本並專注於最強勢的資產來穩定地位。該公司尚未針對 Xbox 如何融入其日益聚焦於人工智慧的整體業務，提出明確的長期策略。隨著微軟持續進行這場「重整」，遊戲產業仍密切關注這些激進措施是否足以讓 Xbox 部門轉虧為盈。對於這些遊戲工作室的員工與粉絲而言，隨著公司持續重組營運，未來幾個月將是充滿高度不確定性的時期。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To try to do too many things at the same time, so that you do not give enough time or energy to any of them.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 同時做太多事，導致精力分散、無法專注。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的慣用語，用來形容資源過度分散。文中：Xbox CEO Asha Sharma explained in a company memo that the business had previously "spread ourselves too thin."

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be dismissed or fired from a job.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 被解僱、被裁員（較委婉的說法）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上是「放手」，在職場語境中是解僱的委婉說法。文中：Kotaku reported that about 25 percent of the employees at Obsidian were let go during this restructuring process.

keyTerms.definitionZh: （方案或選項）被提出來討論、在考慮範圍內。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於商業討論，指某個選項仍列入考慮。文中：Yoshio Osaki, the CEO of the market research firm IDG Intelligence, noted that while all options might be on the table, the current market environment makes a large purchase less likely.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To withdraw or reduce involvement in an activity.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指企業減少投入資金或活動。文中：He observed that other major companies, such as Tencent and NetEase, have recently been stepping back from large investments in the gaming industry rather than expanding.

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: Microsoft’s Xbox reset is pivoting Obsidian to make Fallout instead of Avowed

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: If Microsoft sold off Xbox, who would even buy it?

previousArticles.title: Microsoft Announces Major Job Cuts and Xbox Restructuring
previousArticles.summaryEn: Microsoft has announced the loss of 4,800 jobs, with a significant portion affecting its Xbox gaming division. The company is undergoing a major restructuring to improve its financial health and focus on its most popular game franchises. As part of these changes, four game studios will become independent or be sold to new owners. Microsoft leadership stated that these moves are necessary to adapt to a fast-changing industry. The company continues to invest heavily in artificial intelligence, which is reshaping how work is done across the organization.

previousArticles.title: Microsoft Announces Major Job Cuts and Xbox Restructuring
previousArticles.summaryEn: Microsoft has announced that it will cut 4,800 jobs, representing about 2% of its global workforce. A large portion of these layoffs will occur within the Xbox gaming division. The company is spinning off four game studios to operate independently. This decision is part of a larger strategy to focus on artificial intelligence and high-profit gaming projects. Leadership stated that these changes are necessary to keep the company competitive in a fast-changing industry.

quiz.question: What specific change is Obsidian Entertainment making as part of Microsoft's new strategy?

options.A: It is expanding its staff by 25 percent to work on new projects.
options.B: It is canceling several planned projects to focus on a new Fallout title.
options.C: It is moving away from the Fallout series to prioritize the Avowed sequel.
options.D: It is being sold by Microsoft to another major gaming company.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，Obsidian Entertainment 為了配合微軟的新策略，取消了包含 Avowed 續作在內的數個專案，轉而投入開發新的 Fallout 系列遊戲。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到的是裁員 25%，而非擴編。選項 C 內容與事實完全相反。選項 D 在文中並未提及 Obsidian 被出售，僅提到它是微軟旗下的工作室。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why is it unlikely that Microsoft will sell the entire Xbox division in the near future?

options.A: Because Microsoft has already decided to focus exclusively on AI.
options.B: Because the Xbox division is currently making more profit than ever before.
options.C: Because the high cost and complexity of the business make finding a suitable buyer difficult.
options.D: Because other companies like Tencent are currently looking to buy large gaming assets.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。綜合第四段與第五段，雖然專家提到出售的可能性，但指出由於 Xbox 業務規模龐大且複雜，且年營收超過 230 億美元，尋找買家非常困難。選項 A 錯誤，文章提到微軟正轉向 AI，但未說 Xbox 已被放棄。選項 B 與文章第一段提到的「業務不健康」矛盾。選項 D 錯誤，文章第五段明確指出 Tencent 等公司目前反而是在減少大型投資。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the phrase 'wholesale divestiture' mean in the context of the Xbox business?

options.A: The complete sale of the entire business division.
options.B: A strategy to increase the price of gaming hardware.
options.C: The process of hiring more staff for AI development.
options.D: A partial reduction in the number of game studios owned.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中括號內明確解釋了 'wholesale divestiture' 即為 'selling off the entire business'（將整個業務賣掉）。選項 B、C 與該詞彙的定義無關。選項 D 描述的是「縮編」或「調整」，而非「wholesale」（全面性、整體性的）出售，因此不符。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of this article?

options.A: To explain the history of the Fallout video game series.
options.B: To provide a guide on how to manage large game development studios.
options.C: To discuss the technical specifications of the new 'Project Helix' console.
options.D: To describe the restructuring of Microsoft's Xbox division and the uncertainty surrounding its future.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。整篇文章的核心在於報導微軟 Xbox 部門目前的「重置」計畫，包括裁員、策略調整以及對未來發展（如是否出售）的分析。選項 A 只是第二段提到的細節例子。選項 B 並非文章目的。選項 C 僅在第三段輕描淡寫提到，並非文章主旨。因此 D 是最能涵蓋全文訊息的選項。
